Class AdditionInstruction

java.lang.Object
dk.tij.registermaschine.core.instructions.api.AbstractInstruction
dk.tij.registermaschine.core.instructions.AdditionInstruction
public final class AdditionInstruction extends AbstractInstruction
Instruction handler that performs integer addition.

This instruction sums all operands marked with the OperandConcept.OPERAND concept and stores the resulting value in the operand marked with OperandConcept.RESULT.

The operation supports both register and immediate operands. Operand values are also resolved using AbstractInstruction.getValueFromOperand(IExecutionContext, ICompiledOperand)

The instruction also updates the processor flags in the IExecutionContext:

  • Negative flag - set if the result is negative
  • Zero flag - set if the result is 0
  • Overflow flag - set if the result exceeds the 32-bit signed integer range

Overflow detection is performed using a long accumulator before the final result is cast to int.

At lest one OperandConcept.RESULT operand must be present. Validation fails otherwise.

Since:
1.0.0
Author:
TiJ

    • AdditionInstruction

  • Method Details

    • validate

      public void validate(ICompiledOperand[] operands)
      Ensures that the instruction contains at least one OperandConcept.RESULT operand.
      Overrides:
      validate in class AbstractInstruction
      Parameters:
      operands - the operands supplied for execution
      Throws:
      RuntimeException - if no result operand is present

    • executeInstruction

      public void executeInstruction(IExecutionContext context, ICompiledOperand[] operands)
      Executes the addition operation.

      All operands with the concept OperandConcept.OPERAND are summed and the resulting value is written to the OperandConcept.RESULT operand.

      If the calculated value exceeds the 32-bit signed integer range, the overflow flag is set.

      Specified by:
      executeInstruction in class AbstractInstruction
      Parameters:
      context - the execution context providing register access and processor state
      operands - the compiled operands participating in the addition operation
